item.title: [7] Magnetic-Field-Driven Insulator-Superconductor Transition in Rhombohedral Graphene
item.description:
Authors: Jian Xie, Zihao Huo, Zhimou Chen, Zaizhe Zhang, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Xi Lin, and Xiaobo Lu

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Jian Xie, Zihao Huo, Zhimou Chen, Zaizhe Zhang, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Xi Lin, and Xiaobo Lu

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: High-quality condensed-matter platform for exploring novel superconducting states and electronic correlations.

Tags: #superconductivity #graphene

Abstract: Author(s): Jian Xie, Zihao Huo, Zhimou Chen, Zaizhe Zhang, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Xi Lin, and Xiaobo Lu Recent studies of rhombohedral multilayer graphene have revealed a variety of superconducting states that can be induced or enhanced by magnetic fields, reinforcing rhombohedral multilayer graphene as a powerful platform for investigating novel superconductivity. Here, we report an insulator-superco… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176505] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jian Xie, Zihao Huo, Zhimou Chen, Zaizhe Zhang, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Xi Lin, and Xiaobo Lu

Recent studies of rhombohedral multilayer graphene have revealed a variety of superconducting states that can be induced or enhanced by magnetic fields, reinforcing rhombohedral multilayer graphene as a powerful platform for investigating novel superconductivity. Here, we report an insulator-superco…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176505] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetic-Field-Driven Insulator-Superconductor Transition in Rhombohedral Graphene
item.dc:creator: Jian Xie, Zihao Huo, Zhimou Chen, Zaizhe Zhang, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Xi Lin, and Xiaobo Lu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Microwave Imaging of Edge Conductivity in Graphene at Charge Neutrality and Quantum Hall States
item.description:
Authors: Hongtao Yan, Chun-Chih Tseng, Anzhuoer Li, Manish Kumar, Kaile Wang, Shizai Chu, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Allan H. MacDonald, Matthew Yankowitz, and Keji Lai

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Hongtao Yan, Chun-Chih Tseng, Anzhuoer Li, Manish Kumar, Kaile Wang, Shizai Chu, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Allan H. MacDonald, Matthew Yankowitz, and Keji Lai

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Electronic structure visualization and transport in topological states relevant to magnetic/topological materials.

Tags: #graphene #topological #antiferromagnet

Abstract: Author(s): Hongtao Yan, Chun-Chih Tseng, Anzhuoer Li, Manish Kumar, Kaile Wang, Shizai Chu, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Allan H. MacDonald, Matthew Yankowitz, and Keji Lai The contrasting edge evolution between ν = 0 , consistent with a canted antiferromagnetic phase, and | ν | ≥ 1 chiral edge channels reveals the microscopic distinction between trivial and topological states. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176603] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hongtao Yan, Chun-Chih Tseng, Anzhuoer Li, Manish Kumar, Kaile Wang, Shizai Chu, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Allan H. MacDonald, Matthew Yankowitz, and Keji Lai

The contrasting edge evolution between ν=0, consistent with a canted antiferromagnetic phase, and |ν|≥1 chiral edge channels reveals the microscopic distinction between trivial and topological states.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176603] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: Microwave Imaging of Edge Conductivity in Graphene at Charge Neutrality and Quantum Hall States
item.dc:creator: Hongtao Yan, Chun-Chih Tseng, Anzhuoer Li, Manish Kumar, Kaile Wang, Shizai Chu, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Allan H. MacDonald, Matthew Yankowitz, and Keji Lai
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [7] Identifying Instabilities with Quantum Geometry in Flat-Band Systems
item.description:
Authors: Jia-Xin Zhang, Wen O. Wang, Leon Balents, and Lucile Savary

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Jia-Xin Zhang, Wen O. Wang, Leon Balents, and Lucile Savary

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Relevant framework for discussing electronic instabilities and order in flat-band systems like kagome metals.

Tags: #flatband #quantumgeometry

Abstract: Author(s): Jia-Xin Zhang, Wen O. Wang, Leon Balents, and Lucile Savary Despite the absence of a well-defined Fermi surface in flat-band systems, a nesting structure related to a geometric quantity constructed from the order parameter and the projection operator onto flat-band states replaces Fermi-surface nesting and determines the most favorable ordering across an arbitrary number of flat bands. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176504] Published Thu Apr 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jia-Xin Zhang, Wen O. Wang, Leon Balents, and Lucile Savary

Despite the absence of a well-defined Fermi surface in flat-band systems, a nesting structure related to a geometric quantity constructed from the order parameter and the projection operator onto flat-band states replaces Fermi-surface nesting and determines the most favorable ordering across an arbitrary number of flat bands.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176504] Published Thu Apr 30, 2026
item.dc:title: Identifying Instabilities with Quantum Geometry in Flat-Band Systems
item.dc:creator: Jia-Xin Zhang, Wen O. Wang, Leon Balents, and Lucile Savary
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Spectroscopic Evidence of Disorder-Induced Quantum Phase Transitions in Monolayer Fe(Te,Se) Superconductor
item.description:
Authors: Guanyang He, Ziqiao Wang, Longxin Pan, Yuxuan Lei, Fa Wang, Yi Liu, Nandini Trivedi, and Jian Wang

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Guanyang He, Ziqiao Wang, Longxin Pan, Yuxuan Lei, Fa Wang, Yi Liu, Nandini Trivedi, and Jian Wang

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Direct experimental study on a related iron-based superconductor with clear electronic structure relevance.

Tags: #superconductivity #electronicstructure

Abstract: Author(s): Guanyang He, Ziqiao Wang, Longxin Pan, Yuxuan Lei, Fa Wang, Yi Liu, Nandini Trivedi, and Jian Wang The superconductor-insulator transition as a paradigm of quantum phase transitions has attracted tremendous interest over the past three decades. While the magnetic field and carrier density can be tuned to drive the transition, the role of disorder in the transition is not well understood due to th… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176001] Published Wed Apr 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Guanyang He, Ziqiao Wang, Longxin Pan, Yuxuan Lei, Fa Wang, Yi Liu, Nandini Trivedi, and Jian Wang

The superconductor-insulator transition as a paradigm of quantum phase transitions has attracted tremendous interest over the past three decades. While the magnetic field and carrier density can be tuned to drive the transition, the role of disorder in the transition is not well understood due to th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176001] Published Wed Apr 29, 2026
item.dc:title: Spectroscopic Evidence of Disorder-Induced Quantum Phase Transitions in Monolayer Fe(Te,Se) Superconductor
item.dc:creator: Guanyang He, Ziqiao Wang, Longxin Pan, Yuxuan Lei, Fa Wang, Yi Liu, Nandini Trivedi, and Jian Wang

item.title: [7] Spin Waves Excited by Hard X-Ray Transient Gratings
item.description:
Authors: Peter R. Miedaner et al.

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Peter R. Miedaner et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Relevant spectroscopic technique for probing magnetic dynamics in quantum materials.

Tags: #spectroscopy #magnetism #dynamics

Abstract: Author(s): Peter R. Miedaner et al. Recent progress in ultrafast x-ray sources helped establish x-rays as an important tool for probing lattice and magnetic dynamics initiated by femtosecond optical pulses. Here, we explore the potential of ultrashort hard x-ray pulses for driving magnetic dynamics. We use a transient grating techniqu… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176701] Published Wed Apr 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Peter R. Miedaner et al.

Recent progress in ultrafast x-ray sources helped establish x-rays as an important tool for probing lattice and magnetic dynamics initiated by femtosecond optical pulses. Here, we explore the potential of ultrashort hard x-ray pulses for driving magnetic dynamics. We use a transient grating techniqu…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176701] Published Wed Apr 29, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin Waves Excited by Hard X-Ray Transient Gratings
item.dc:creator: Peter R. Miedaner et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [7] Coherent Subgap Transport in Spin-Split Josephson Junctions
item.description:
Authors: David Caldevilla-Asenjo, Gorm Ole Steffensen, Sara Catalano, Alberto Hijano, Maxim Ilyn, Celia Rogero, Ramon Aguado, F. Sebastian Bergeret, and Alfredo Levy Yeyati

Last/corresponding-author proxy: David Caldevilla-Asenjo, Gorm Ole Steffensen, Sara Catalano, Alberto Hijano, Maxim Ilyn, Celia Rogero, Ramon Aguado, F. Sebastian Bergeret, and Alfredo Levy Yeyati

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of spin-split superconducting systems relevant to magnetic topological materials.

Tags: #superconductivity #transport #spin-splitting

Abstract: Author(s): David Caldevilla-Asenjo, Gorm Ole Steffensen, Sara Catalano, Alberto Hijano, Maxim Ilyn, Celia Rogero, Ramon Aguado, F. Sebastian Bergeret, and Alfredo Levy Yeyati We report the first experimental observation of subgap transport in ferromagnetic insulator–superconductor–insulator–superconductor junctions realized in $\mathrm{EuS}/\mathrm{Al}/\mathrm{AlOx}/\mathrm{Al}$ vertical stacks. Differential conductance measurements reveal multiple Andreev reflection pea… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176301] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): David Caldevilla-Asenjo, Gorm Ole Steffensen, Sara Catalano, Alberto Hijano, Maxim Ilyn, Celia Rogero, Ramon Aguado, F. Sebastian Bergeret, and Alfredo Levy Yeyati

We report the first experimental observation of subgap transport in ferromagnetic insulator–superconductor–insulator–superconductor junctions realized in EuS/Al/AlOx/Al vertical stacks. Differential conductance measurements reveal multiple Andreev reflection peaks, with odd-order peaks split by the …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176301] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.dc:title: Coherent Subgap Transport in Spin-Split Josephson Junctions
item.dc:creator: David Caldevilla-Asenjo, Gorm Ole Steffensen, Sara Catalano, Alberto Hijano, Maxim Ilyn, Celia Rogero, Ramon Aguado, F. Sebastian Bergeret, and Alfredo Levy Yeyati
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [7] Superconductivity of Incoherent Electrons near the Relativistic Mott Transition in Twisted Dirac Materials
item.description:
Authors: Veronika C. Stangier, Mathias S. Scheurer, Daniel E. Sheehy, and Jörg Schmalian

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Veronika C. Stangier, Mathias S. Scheurer, Daniel E. Sheehy, and Jörg Schmalian

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Connects Dirac materials and Mott physics, relevant to the user's focus on topological/Dirac systems.

Tags: #Dirac #superconductivity #Mott

Abstract: Author(s): Veronika C. Stangier, Mathias S. Scheurer, Daniel E. Sheehy, and Jörg Schmalian Superconductivity emerges in an unexpected way in twisted two-dimensional materials like graphene, even when there are essentially no mobile charge carriers at zero temperature. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176501] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Veronika C. Stangier, Mathias S. Scheurer, Daniel E. Sheehy, and Jörg Schmalian

Superconductivity emerges in an unexpected way in twisted two-dimensional materials like graphene, even when there are essentially no mobile charge carriers at zero temperature.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176501] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.dc:title: Superconductivity of Incoherent Electrons near the Relativistic Mott Transition in Twisted Dirac Materials
item.dc:creator: Veronika C. Stangier, Mathias S. Scheurer, Daniel E. Sheehy, and Jörg Schmalian
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Elastic Response and Instabilities of Anomalous Hall Crystals
item.description:
Authors: Félix Desrochers, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Joe Huxford, Adarsh S. Patri, T. Senthil, and Yong Baek Kim

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Félix Desrochers, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Joe Huxford, Adarsh S. Patri, T. Senthil, and Yong Baek Kim

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Addresses anomalous Hall effects and translation symmetry breaking, highly relevant to kagome systems.

Tags: #anomalousHall #symmetrybreaking #topology

Abstract: Author(s): Félix Desrochers, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Joe Huxford, Adarsh S. Patri, T. Senthil, and Yong Baek Kim Anomalous Hall crystals (AHCs) are exotic phases of matter that simultaneously break continuous translation symmetry and exhibit the quantum anomalous Hall effect. AHCs have recently been proposed to explain the observation of an integer quantum anomalous Hall phase in a multilayer graphene system. … [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166503] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Félix Desrochers, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Joe Huxford, Adarsh S. Patri, T. Senthil, and Yong Baek Kim

Anomalous Hall crystals (AHCs) are exotic phases of matter that simultaneously break continuous translation symmetry and exhibit the quantum anomalous Hall effect. AHCs have recently been proposed to explain the observation of an integer quantum anomalous Hall phase in a multilayer graphene system. …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166503] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Elastic Response and Instabilities of Anomalous Hall Crystals
item.dc:creator: Félix Desrochers, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Joe Huxford, Adarsh S. Patri, T. Senthil, and Yong Baek Kim
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [7] Orbital Magnetization of Correlated States in Twisted Bilayer Transition Metal Dichalcogenides
item.description:
Authors: Xiaoyu Liu, Chong Wang, Haoran Chen, Xiao-Wei Zhang, Ting Cao, and Di Xiao

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Xiaoyu Liu, Chong Wang, Haoran Chen, Xiao-Wei Zhang, Ting Cao, and Di Xiao

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of orbital magnetization relevant for magneto-ARPES and correlated systems.

Tags: #magneto-ARPES #correlated #magnetization

Abstract: Author(s): Xiaoyu Liu, Chong Wang, Haoran Chen, Xiao-Wei Zhang, Ting Cao, and Di Xiao The modern theory of orbital magnetization, derived for noninteracting electrons, applied to strongly correlated states remains valid for Hartree–Fock states, so can be used to interpret magnetization in interacting moiré systems. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166606] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiaoyu Liu, Chong Wang, Haoran Chen, Xiao-Wei Zhang, Ting Cao, and Di Xiao

The modern theory of orbital magnetization, derived for noninteracting electrons, applied to strongly correlated states remains valid for Hartree–Fock states, so can be used to interpret magnetization in interacting moiré systems.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166606] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Orbital Magnetization of Correlated States in Twisted Bilayer Transition Metal Dichalcogenides
item.dc:creator: Xiaoyu Liu, Chong Wang, Haoran Chen, Xiao-Wei Zhang, Ting Cao, and Di Xiao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Quantum Transport Spectroscopy of Pseudomagnetic Field in Graphene
item.description:
Authors: Divya Sahani, Sunit Das, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Amit Agarwal, and Aveek Bid

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Divya Sahani, Sunit Das, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Amit Agarwal, and Aveek Bid

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Transport spectroscopy of strain and pseudofields; highly relevant to Berry curvature/topological materials.

Tags: #Dirac #transport #Berrycurvature

Abstract: Author(s): Divya Sahani, Sunit Das, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Amit Agarwal, and Aveek Bid A quantitative detection of strain-induced pseudomagnetic fields in graphene resolves a challenge in Dirac materials by showing that valley-antisymmetric gauge fields imprint a universal, symmetry-protected beating signature on the Shubnikov-de Haas oscillations in the magnetotransport. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166604] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Divya Sahani, Sunit Das, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Amit Agarwal, and Aveek Bid

A quantitative detection of strain-induced pseudomagnetic fields in graphene resolves a challenge in Dirac materials by showing that valley-antisymmetric gauge fields imprint a universal, symmetry-protected beating signature on the Shubnikov-de Haas oscillations in the magnetotransport.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166604] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Transport Spectroscopy of Pseudomagnetic Field in Graphene
item.dc:creator: Divya Sahani, Sunit Das, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Amit Agarwal, and Aveek Bid
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [7] Floquet Spin Splitting and Spin Generation in Antiferromagnets
item.description:
Authors: Bo Li, Ding-Fu Shao, and Alexey A. Kovalev

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Bo Li, Ding-Fu Shao, and Alexey A. Kovalev

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of spin generation in antiferromagnets; connects to altermagnetism/TRSB interests.

Tags: #antiferromagnet #altermagnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Bo Li, Ding-Fu Shao, and Alexey A. Kovalev Polarized light lifts spin degeneracy in conventional antiferromagnets without requiring spin-orbit coupling and generates steady-state pure spin current. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166701] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bo Li, Ding-Fu Shao, and Alexey A. Kovalev

Polarized light lifts spin degeneracy in conventional antiferromagnets without requiring spin-orbit coupling and generates steady-state pure spin current.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166701] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Floquet Spin Splitting and Spin Generation in Antiferromagnets
item.dc:creator: Bo Li, Ding-Fu Shao, and Alexey A. Kovalev
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Lattice Excitations with Finite Polarization and Magnetization
item.description:
Authors: Mike Pols, Carl P. Romao, and Dominik M. Juraschek

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Mike Pols, Carl P. Romao, and Dominik M. Juraschek

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of excitations in multiferroic systems; relevant to TRSB/magnetism.

Tags: #magnon #TRSB #magnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Mike Pols, Carl P. Romao, and Dominik M. Juraschek Ferrons are a type of quasiparticle corresponding to elementary excitations of the ferroelectric order. Analogously to how magnons modulate and transport magnetization, ferrons modulate and transport electric polarization. Here, we introduce multiferrons as elementary excitations with both electric … [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166801] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mike Pols, Carl P. Romao, and Dominik M. Juraschek

Ferrons are a type of quasiparticle corresponding to elementary excitations of the ferroelectric order. Analogously to how magnons modulate and transport magnetization, ferrons modulate and transport electric polarization. Here, we introduce multiferrons as elementary excitations with both electric …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166801] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Lattice Excitations with Finite Polarization and Magnetization
item.dc:creator: Mike Pols, Carl P. Romao, and Dominik M. Juraschek
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Compatible Instability: Gauge Constraints of Elasticity Inherited by Electronic Nematic Criticality
item.description:
Authors: W. Joe Meese and Rafael M. Fernandes

Last/corresponding-author proxy: W. Joe Meese and Rafael M. Fernandes

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical advancement in electronic nematicity relevant to CDW systems and nematic order.

Tags: #nematicity #CDW

Abstract: Author(s): W. Joe Meese and Rafael M. Fernandes A 19th-century theory of elasticity inspires a new way to analyze a quantum phase transition that has become central to modern quantum materials research. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166501] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): W. Joe Meese and Rafael M. Fernandes

A 19th-century theory of elasticity inspires a new way to analyze a quantum phase transition that has become central to modern quantum materials research.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166501] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Compatible Instability: Gauge Constraints of Elasticity Inherited by Electronic Nematic Criticality
item.dc:creator: W. Joe Meese and Rafael M. Fernandes
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials
